package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs
package.doc:
A distributed implementation of {@link org.apache.hadoop.fs.FileSystem}. This is loosely modelled after Google's GFS.

The most important difference is that unlike GFS, Hadoop DFS files have strictly one writer at any one time. Bytes are always appended to the end of the writer's stream. There is no notion of "record appends" or "mutations" that are then checked or reordered. Writers simply emit a byte stream. That byte stream is guaranteed to be stored in the order written.

class.name: Feature
class.doc:
Enums for features that change the layout version before rolling upgrade is supported.

To add a new layout version:
  • Define a new enum constant with a short enum name, the new layout version and description of the added feature.
  • When adding a layout version with an ancestor that is not same as its immediate predecessor, use the constructor where a specific ancestor can be passed.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.protocol.datatransfer
package.doc: This package contains classes related to hdfs data transfer protocol.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.protocol.datatransfer.sasl
package.doc: Classes for data transfer SASL implementation.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.protocolPB

interface.name: JournalNodeMXBean

method.name: getJournalsStatus
method.doc:
Get status information (e.g., whether formatted) of JournalNode's journals.
@return A string presenting status for each journal

method.name: getHostAndPort
method.doc:
Get host and port of JournalNode.
@return colon separated host and port.

method.name: getClusterIds
getVersion
method.doc:
Gets the version of Hadoop.
@return the version of Hadoop.

method.name: getJNStartedTimeInMillis
method.doc:
Get the start time of the JournalNode.
@return the start time of the JournalNode.

method.name: getStorageInfos
method.doc:
Get the list of the storage infos of JournalNode's journals. Storage infos include layout version, namespace id, cluster id and creation time of the File system state.
@return the list of storage infos associated with journals.

interface.doc: This is the JMX management interface for JournalNode information

class.name: InMemoryAliasMap

method.name: transferForBootstrap
completeBootstrapTransfer

class.doc: InMemoryAliasMap is an implementation of the InMemoryAliasMapProtocol for use with LevelDB.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.balancer

class.name: ExitStatus

method.name: getExitCode
method.doc: @return the command line exit code.

class.doc: Exit status - The values associated with each exit status is directly mapped to the process's exit code in command line.

package.doc: The Balancer is a tool for balancing the data across the storage devices of an HDFS cluster. Over time, the data in the HDFS storage can become skewed, The Balancer moves data blocks between DataNodes to balance data distribution.

class.name: Reason
class.doc: The corruption reason code

interface.name: BlockAlias
interface.doc: Interface used to load provided blocks.

class.name: BlockUCState
class.doc: States, which a block can go through while it is under construction.

class.name: FileRegion

constructor.name: FileRegion
constructor.type: long, org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path, long, long, long, byte[]

class.doc: This class is used to represent provided blocks that are file regions, i.e., can be described using (path, offset, length).

class.name: LockLevel
class.doc: Acquire block pool level and volume level lock first if you want to acquire dir lock. Or only acquire block pool level lock. There are several locking sequential patterns as below: 1. block pool 2. block pool -> volume 3. block pool level -> volume -> dir

class.name: NamenodeRole
class.doc: Defines the NameNode role.

class.name: NodeType
class.doc: Type of the node

class.name: ReplicaState

method.name: getState
read
write
method.doc: Write to out

class.doc: Block replica states, which it can go through while being constructed.

class.name: RollingUpgradeStartupOption
class.doc: Startup options for rolling upgrade.

class.name: StartupOption
class.doc: Startup options

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.common.blockaliasmap

class.name: BlockAliasMap

method.name: getReader
getWriter
refresh
method.doc: Refresh the alias map. @throws IOException

class.doc: An abstract class used to read and write block maps for provided blocks.

package.doc: The AliasMap defines mapping of PROVIDED HDFS blocks to data in remote storage systems.

class.name: LevelDBFileRegionAliasMap
class.doc: A LevelDB based implementation of {@link BlockAliasMap}.

class.name: TextFileRegionAliasMap
class.doc: This class is used for block maps stored as text files, with a specified delimiter.

class.name: Feature
class.doc:
Enums for features that change the layout version.

To add a new layout version:
  • Define a new enum constant with a short enum name, the new layout version and description of the added feature.
  • When adding a layout version with an ancestor that is not same as its immediate predecessor, use the constructor where a specific ancestor can be passed.

class.name: OPERATION
class.doc: Lists the types of file system operations. Passed to the IO hooks so implementations can choose behavior based on specific operations.

class.name: BlockDirFilter
class.doc: Filter for block file names stored on the file system volumes.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.diskbalancer

class.name: Result
class.doc: Results returned by the RPC layer of DiskBalancer.

package.doc:
Disk Balancer connects to a {@link org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.datanode .DataNode} and attempts to spread data across all volumes evenly.
This is achieved by :
1) Calculating the average data that should be on a set of volumes grouped by the type. For example, how much data should be on each volume of SSDs on a machine.
2) Once we know the average data that is expected to be on a volume we move data from volumes with higher than average load to volumes with less than average load.
3) Disk Balancer operates against data nodes which are live and operational.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.diskbalancer.command
package.doc: Commands for disk balancer command line tool.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.diskbalancer.connectors
package.doc:
Connectors package is a set of logical connectors that connect to various data sources to read the hadoop cluster information.
We currently have 3 connectors in this package. They are
DBNameNodeConnector - This uses the connector from the original balancer package to connect to a real hadoop cluster.
JsonNodeConnector - This connects to a file and reads the data about a cluster. We can generate a cluster json from a real cluster using the diskBalancer tool or hand-craft it. There are some sample Json files checked in under test/resources/diskBalancer directory.
NullConnector - This is an in-memory connector that is useful in testing. we can crate dataNodes on the fly and attach to this connector and ask the diskBalancer Cluster to read data from this source.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.diskbalancer.datamodel
package.doc:
Disk Balancer Data Model is the Data Model for the cluster that Disk Balancer is working against. This information is read directly from NameNode or from a user supplied json model file.
Here is the overview of the model maintained by diskBalancer.
DiskBalancerCluster is a list of DiskBalancerDataNodes. DiskBalancerDataNodes is a collection of DiskBalancerVolumeSets DiskBalancerVolumeSets is a collection of DiskBalancerVolumes DiskBalancerVolumes represents actual volumes on DataNodes.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.diskbalancer.planner
org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.mover
package.doc: Mover is a data migration tool for tiered storage. It scans provided paths in HDFS to check if the block placement satisfies the storage policy. For the blocks violating the storage policy, it moves the replicas to a different storage type in order to fulfill the storage policy requirement.

class.name: AclEntryStatusFormat
class.doc:
Class to pack an AclEntry into an integer.
An ACL entry is represented by a 32-bit integer in Big Endian format.
Note: this format is used both in-memory and on-disk. Changes will be incompatible.

method.name: initialize
method.doc:
Called during initialization of the logger.
@param conf The configuration object.

method.name: logAuditEvent

class.name: Content
class.doc: The content types such as file, directory and symlink to be computed.

class.name: DefaultAuditLogger

field.name: callerContextMaxLen
field.doc: The maximum bytes a caller context string can have.

field.name: logTokenTrackingId
field.doc: adds a tracking ID for all audit log events.

field.name: debugCmdSet
field.doc: List of commands to provide debug messages.

class.doc: This class provides an interface for Namenode and Router to Audit events information. This class can be extended and can be used when no access logger is defined in the config file.

class.name: Feature
class.doc:
Enums for features that change the layout version.

To add a new layout version:
  • Define a new enum constant with a short enum name, the new layout version and description of the added feature.
  • When adding a layout version with an ancestor that is not same as its immediate predecessor, use the constructor where a specific ancestor can be passed.
  • Specify a minimum compatible layout version. The minimum compatible layout version is the earliest prior version to which a downgrade is possible after initiating rolling upgrade. If the feature cannot satisfy compatibility with any prior version, then set its minimum compatible layout version to itself to indicate that downgrade is impossible. Satisfying compatibility might require adding logic to the new feature to reject operations or handle them differently while rolling upgrade is in progress. In general, it's possible to satisfy compatibility for downgrade if the new feature just involves adding new edit log ops. Deeper structural changes, such as changing the way we place files in the metadata directories, might be incompatible. Feature implementations should strive for compatibility, because it's in the best interest of our users to support downgrade.

class.name: HdfsAuditLogger

method.name: logAuditEvent
logAuditEvent
method.doc: Same as {@link #logAuditEvent(boolean, String, InetAddress, String, String, String, FileStatus, CallerContext, UserGroupInformation, DelegationTokenSecretManager)} without {@link CallerContext} information.

class.doc: Extension of {@link AuditLogger}.

method.name: start
method.doc: Initialize the provider. This method is called at NameNode startup time.

method.name: stop
method.doc: Shutdown the provider. This method is called at NameNode shutdown time.

method.name: getExternalAccessControlEnforcer

class.name: NameNodeDirType
class.doc: Implementation of StorageDirType specific to namenode storage A Storage directory could be of type IMAGE which stores only fsimage, or of type EDITS which stores edits or of type IMAGE_AND_EDITS which stores both fsimage and edits.

class.name: NameNodeFile
class.doc: The filenames used for storing the images.

class.name: OperationCategory
class.doc: Categories of operations supported by the namenode.

class.name: Quota

method.name: isViolated
method.doc: Is quota violated? The quota is violated if quota is set and usage > quota.

class.doc: Quota types.

class.name: SectionName
class.doc: Supported section name. The order of the enum determines the order of loading.

class.name: SerialNumberManager
class.doc: Manage name-to-serial-number maps for various string tables.

class.name: XAttrFormat
class.doc:
Class to pack XAttrs into byte[].
Note: this format is used both in-memory and on-disk. Changes will be incompatible.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.namenode.visitor
package.doc: Use the visitor pattern to visit namespace tree.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.server.sps.metrics
package.doc: This package provides the ability to expose external SPS metrics to JMX.

class.name: RwLockMode
class.doc: This lock mode is used for FGL.
